evals.prompt: I need a new STM32 firmware project for a smart sensor node. I'm on macOS Apple Silicon. Use the default STM32G030C8T6 target, create the project in /tmp/embedded_eval_smart_sensor, and set up CMake, Ninja, arm-none-eabi-gcc, clang-format, clang-tidy, OpenOCD, and Cortex-Debug in VS Code.
evals.expected_output: A greenfield STM32G030C8T6 firmware project with CMakePresets.json, toolchain file, startup code, linker script, layered src/app and src/bsp, build/format/analyze/flash/openocd scripts, .vscode configs, Dockerfile, and GitHub Actions workflow.
expectations: CMakeLists.txt targets the arm-none-eabi-gcc toolchain and produces an ELF
cmake/arm-none-eabi-gcc.cmake sets CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME Generic and uses arm-none-eabi-gcc
CMakePresets.json contains stm32-debug, stm32-release, and stm32-analyze presets
startup/startup.c provides a vector table and Reset_Handler
ldscripts/smart_sensor_flash.ld defines FLASH and RAM regions matching STM32G030C8T6 (64K flash, 8K RAM)
.vscode/launch.json has a cortex-debug configuration using OpenOCD
scripts/flash.sh and scripts/openocd_server.sh reference stlink and stm32g0x cfg files
README.md mentions macOS Apple Silicon host profile and build/flash commands

evals.prompt: Create an STM32 firmware project in /tmp/embedded_eval_motor_ctrl for the NUCLEO-F446RE board (STM32F446RE, Cortex-M4, 512 KiB flash, 128 KiB RAM, FPU fpv4-sp-d16, hard float). Use OpenOCD target stm32f4x and ST-Link. Include a layered application with app/, bsp/, and drivers/ directories, plus CMSIS/HAL placeholders in the README.
evals.expected_output: A custom STM32F446RE firmware scaffold with correct CPU/FPU/float-abi flags, linker script sizes, OpenOCD config, and layered source tree.
expectations: CMakeLists.txt passes -mcpu=cortex-m4 -mthumb -mfpu=fpv4-sp-d16 -mfloat-abi=hard
CMakePresets.json has stm32-debug, stm32-release, and stm32-analyze presets
ldscripts/motor_ctrl_flash.ld has 512K flash and 128K RAM lengths
.vscode/launch.json references interface/stlink.cfg and target/stm32f4x.cfg
src/ contains app/, bsp/, and drivers/ subdirectories
README.md notes the STM32F446RE target and suggests CMSIS/HAL integration

evals.prompt: Modernize an existing firmware project in /tmp/embedded_eval_cubemx_legacy. It currently has a Keil .uvprojx, CubeMX-generated Src/Inc folders with HAL files, and an ST-Link debug setup. Convert it to CMake + Ninja + arm-none-eabi-gcc, add clang-format/clang-tidy, OpenOCD flash/debug scripts, and VS Code Cortex-Debug. Preserve the existing HAL-based application code.
evals.expected_output: The existing CubeMX/Keil firmware is modernized with CMake, presets, toolchain file, clang-format/tidy, OpenOCD scripts, and VS Code configs while keeping the HAL application intact.
expectations: Existing Src/ and Inc/ HAL application files are preserved, not deleted
CMakeLists.txt is added and configured for arm-none-eabi-gcc
cmake/arm-none-eabi-gcc.cmake is created
CMakePresets.json with stm32-debug, stm32-release, and stm32-analyze is created
.clang-format, .clang-tidy, and .clangd are added
.vscode/launch.json uses cortex-debug with OpenOCD
scripts/flash.sh and scripts/openocd_server.sh are added
README.md documents the new build/flash workflow
